description: ScentLib's Layer 1 taxonomy is a synthesis of three established olfactory ontologies: the Dravnieks 1985 NMF-derived 10 categories (primary source), the IAWPRC 1986 environmental odor wheel (8 categories), and the IFRA fragrance industry taxonomy. Where these overlap, we follow the Dravnieks NMF clustering. Where they diverge or have gaps (notably for sensor artifacts and industrial odors), ScentLib defines its own categories, marked with source='scentlib'. This file is the authority for layer1_category validation in scent_schema.json.

primary_sources.full: Carrasco et al. (2016). Multivariate Analysis and Classification of 146 Odor Character Descriptors. Chemosensory Perception. DOI: 10.1007/s12078-021-09288-1
primary_sources.note: PCA/NMF on the Dravnieks 1985 Atlas (160 samples × 146 descriptors) yielded 10 base perceptual clusters.

primary_sources.full: IAWPRC (1986). Environmental odor wheel: 8 major categories for natural water odors.

primary_sources.note: Reference for fragrance-industry sub-categories (Animalic, Musk, Leather).

categories.name: Floral
categories.description: Flower-derived scents: rose, jasmine, violet, lavender, lily, geranium, cologne, perfumery.

categories.name: Fruity
categories.description: Non-citrus sweet fruits: apple, peach, banana, berry, pear, apricot, grape.
categories.note: Separated from 'citrus' per Dravnieks NMF clustering — fruity and citrus occupy distinct perceptual clusters.

categories.name: Citrus
categories.description: Acidic citrus fruits: lemon, orange, grapefruit, lime, bergamot.
categories.note: Distinct cluster from 'fruity' in Dravnieks NMF. Acidic/tart character separates them perceptually.

categories.name: Woody & Earthy
categories.description: Wood, roots, damp soil, moss, resin: sandalwood, cedar, geosmin, musty, moldy.

categories.name: Spicy & Warm
categories.description: Warm culinary spices and resins: cinnamon, clove, pepper, vanilla, sweet, burnt/smoky.
categories.note: Includes the 'sweet' and 'burnt/smoky' Dravnieks NMF clusters which are perceptually adjacent to warm spices.

categories.name: Minty & Fresh
categories.description: Cool, refreshing, camphoraceous, green: mint, eucalyptus, cool, fresh, herbal, grass.
categories.note: Renamed from 'green_herbal' to match the Dravnieks NMF cluster label 'Minty/Refreshing'. Green and herbal are sub-descriptors of this cluster, not a separate top-level family.

categories.name: Animalic
categories.description: Animal-derived notes, musk, leather, sweat: civet, castoreum, ambergris, leather.
categories.note: Not a top-level Dravnieks NMF cluster. Sourced from IFRA fragrance taxonomy. Present in Dravnieks as sub-descriptors within putrid or spicy clusters.

categories.name: Chemical & Industrial
categories.description: Solvents, petroleum, plastic, rubber, ozone, paint, gasoline: ethanol, chlorine, benzene.
categories.note: Maps to the 'Chemical smell' NMF cluster. Also aligns with IAWPRC 'Chemical' category.

categories.name: Savory & Food
categories.description: Non-sweet culinary smells: grilled meat, cheese, bread, garlic, onion, popcorn, coffee.
categories.note: Not a top-level Dravnieks NMF cluster. ScentLib addition to cover food-adjacent odors captured by sensors in kitchen/food environments. Descriptors draw from Dravnieks 'bakery', 'meaty', 'garlic' sub-clusters.

categories.name: Putrid & Decay
categories.description: Decaying organic matter, sulfurous, fecal, ammoniacal: rotten egg, sulfur, fishy, ammonia.
categories.note: Maps to two distinct Dravnieks NMF clusters: 'Decomposed' and 'Rancid'. Merged into one ScentLib category for simplicity at layer1; split at layer2 if needed.

categories.name: Medicinal
categories.description: Pharmaceutical, antiseptic, disinfectant: alcohol, camphor, phenolic, iodine.
categories.note: Sourced from IAWPRC 'Medicinal' category. Not a distinct NMF cluster in Dravnieks (descriptors scatter between chemical and minty). Kept as ScentLib top-level for practical relevance in healthcare/lab sensor contexts.

categories.name: Unclassified
categories.description: Unknown scent, sensor artifact, complex mixture not mappable to any single category, or novel synthetic compound.
categories.note: ScentLib-specific category. Required for sensor data where chemical identity is unknown, or for mixtures too complex to classify.
